sense.definition: a building or a group of buildings in which individual units are owned privately, while common areas and facilities such as hallways, elevators, etc. are owned and managed by all residents

examples.x: She bought a condominium in the downtown area for its convenient location and amenities.
The condominium association holds regular meetings to discuss maintenance and community issues.
Residents of the condominium enjoy access to shared facilities such as a swimming pool and fitness center.
Condominiums are popular among urban dwellers seeking homeownership with shared maintenance responsibilities.
The condominium fee covers maintenance costs for common areas and services provided by the homeowners' association.

sense.definition: a hut, shelter, or cabin, usually at a swimming pool or beach

examples.x: The resort's cabanas lined the poolside, offering guests a private retreat to relax in the shade.
They rented a cabana for the day at the beach club, complete with comfortable loungers and a personal attendant.
The tropical cabana was adorned with colorful fabrics and surrounded by lush greenery, creating a paradise-like setting.
Families gathered in the cabana for a barbecue, enjoying delicious food and laughter under the sun.
As the sun began to set, they lit candles in the cabana, transforming it into a romantic oasis by the sea.

sense.definition: a place that has a few rooms for people to live in, normally part of a building that has other such places on each floor

examples.x: He rented an apartment in the city to be closer to his workplace.
He uses the apartment's laundry facilities to wash his clothes.
She invited her friends over to her apartment for a movie night.
She lives in a small apartment on the third floor of the building.
The apartment has a secure entry system.

sense.definition: a tall building with one or several apartments built on each floor

examples.x: Residents of the apartment building enjoyed access to shared facilities such as a fitness center, rooftop terrace, and communal lounge.
The apartment building loomed tall in the city skyline, offering modern living spaces and convenient amenities.
Families moved into the newly constructed apartment building, attracted by its spacious floor plans and proximity to schools and shopping centers.
The apartment building underwent renovations to update its interiors and enhance energy efficiency, improving the quality of life for tenants.
Tenants gathered for a community barbecue in the courtyard of the apartment building, fostering a sense of camaraderie among neighbors.

sense.definition: a residential dwelling designed and intended for occupancy by a single household or family

examples.x: After years of apartment living, they finally bought a single-family home in the suburbs.
The single-family home came with a large backyard where the kids could play.
Many families prefer a single-family home because it provides more freedom and independence.
They moved from a cramped apartment to a beautiful single-family home near the beach.
The neighborhood is mostly made up of single-family homes with front lawns and driveways.

sense.definition: a type of house that is typically multi-storied and attached to other similar houses in a row or block, often found in urban areas

examples.x: The town house was built in a row with five other homes, all sharing side walls.
Each town house has its own small garden at the back.

sense.definition: a factory-built narrow house that is placed in a fixed location for people to live in
an apartment on top of a tall building

examples.x: She moved into a penthouse with stunning views of the city skyline.
The penthouse includes a private terrace, a hot tub, and floor-to-ceiling windows.
The penthouse is considered the most exclusive residence in the building.
He threw a lavish party in his penthouse to celebrate his promotion.
They stayed in a penthouse suite during their vacation, enjoying unparalleled luxury.

sense.definition: a residential building that contains two separate living units or residences, typically stacked on top of each other or side by side

examples.x: The duplex had two separate apartments, each with its own entrance.
They decided to live in a duplex to have more space without paying for a detached house.
The duplex offered the ideal balance of independence and affordability.
The couple moved into a duplex to save on rent while still enjoying some privacy.
The duplex was located in a quiet neighborhood with easy access to public transport.

sense.definition: a small wooden house or shelter built in a forest or the mountains

examples.x: Hikers sought refuge in the remote cabin during a sudden snowstorm, huddling around the fireplace for warmth.
The cozy cabin nestled among towering pine trees, its smokestack releasing wisps of woodsmoke into the crisp mountain air.
Families gathered around the campfire outside the cabin, roasting marshmallows and sharing stories under the starlit sky.
The secluded cabin provided a quiet sanctuary for writers and artists seeking inspiration in nature's beauty.

sense.definition: a wooden house with a steep sloping roof, often found in mountainous areas in Switzerland

examples.x: The family rented a cozy chalet in the Swiss Alps for their winter vacation.
The chalet nestled among the pine trees offered stunning views of the snow-capped mountains.
She dreamt of owning a charming chalet overlooking a pristine alpine lake.
Guests gathered around the fireplace in the chalet's common area to unwind after a day of skiing.
The chalet's wooden beams and sloping roof added to its alpine charm.

sense.definition: a large country house or mansion, typically of French origin, often associated with luxury, grandeur, and historical significance

examples.x: The grand chateau stood atop a hill, overlooking sprawling vineyards and offering breathtaking views of the countryside.
As a wedding venue, the historic chateau provided a romantic backdrop for couples exchanging vows amidst ornate gardens and elegant ballrooms.
The chateau's opulent interiors were adorned with priceless artwork, antique furnishings, and intricate tapestries, reflecting its rich history and noble heritage.
Visitors marveled at the chateau's impressive architecture, with its soaring towers, turrets, and imposing stone façade evoking the splendor of a bygone era.
The chateau served as a luxurious retreat for royalty and aristocrats, hosting lavish banquets, soirées, and hunting parties in its sprawling estate.

sense.definition: a big home in the countryside, often with large grounds or gardens

examples.x: The country house stood majestically amidst rolling hills and lush greenery, its expansive grounds perfect for outdoor activities.
Families escaped to their country house on weekends, enjoying the tranquility and natural beauty of the countryside.
The country house was steeped in history, with generations of owners adding their own touches to the sprawling estate.
Artists and writers found inspiration in the serene surroundings of the country house, capturing the beauty of nature in their works.
The country house featured a charming farmhouse kitchen, where guests gathered for hearty meals prepared with locally sourced ingredients.

sense.definition: a small house, particularly one that is situated in the countryside or a village

examples.x: They spent the weekend at a charming cottage by the lake.
The artist rented a cozy cottage in the mountains for inspiration.
Her grandparents live in a quaint cottage surrounded by gardens.
The old cottage at the edge of the village has been restored beautifully.
They dreamed of retiring to a little cottage in the English countryside.

sense.definition: a single-story residential dwelling with a long, low profile and an open floor plan

examples.x: The ranch house sat nestled among rolling hills, its wide veranda offering sweeping views of the countryside.
They hosted a barbecue in the backyard of their ranch house, inviting friends and family to enjoy good food and company.
The ranch house was a sprawling one-story structure with a rustic charm, featuring exposed wooden beams and a stone fireplace.
They spent weekends riding horses and exploring the trails near their ranch house, embracing the outdoor lifestyle.
With its spacious layout and open floor plan, the ranch house was perfect for entertaining guests and accommodating large gatherings.

sense.definition: a shelter equipped with strong walls, often built underground, to protect soldiers or guns

examples.x: The soldiers took cover in the bunker during the intense bombardment.
The entrance to the underground bunker was well hidden from enemy sight.
The troops were briefed on the emergency evacuation plan from the bunker.
During the war, the bunker provided crucial protection from aerial attacks.
After the attack, the survivors regrouped in the bunker to plan their next move.

sense.definition: a small house, particularly one in the mountains or the countryside, that is made of thick pieces of wood

examples.x: They spent their summer weekends in a cozy log cabin by the lake, surrounded by trees.
After years of living in the city, they decided to move to a log cabin in the woods for a quieter life.
Their family’s log cabin was a perfect place to escape and enjoy the beauty of nature.
They renovated the old log cabin to include modern amenities while maintaining its rustic charm.
The log cabin was built by hand using timber from the surrounding forest.

sense.definition: a very large and impressive house

examples.x: The billionaire lives in a luxurious mansion overlooking the ocean.
They were invited to a gala event hosted at a historic mansion in the countryside.
The mansion has ten bedrooms, a swimming pool, and a large garden.
The old mansion has been converted into a boutique hotel.
He always dreamed of owning a mansion with a grand staircase and a library.

sense.definition: a large and impressively designed house that is often the official house of a powerful individual such as a president or king

examples.x: The royal palace is open to the public, allowing visitors to explore its grand halls and beautiful gardens.
The president's palace features modern architecture while still paying homage to the country's historical roots.
She marveled at the intricate details of the palace, from the ornate ceilings to the exquisite chandeliers.
Many films and documentaries highlight the history of famous palaces, showcasing their cultural significance.
The palace was adorned with artworks from renowned artists, making it a true treasure trove of culture and history.

sense.definition: a country house that has a large garden, particularly the one located in southern Europe or warm regions

examples.x: They spent their summer vacation in a beautiful villa overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, surrounded by lush gardens.
The family rented a villa in Tuscany, where they could enjoy the quiet countryside and local wines.
The villa was spacious, with several bedrooms, a private pool, and a large terrace perfect for evening dinners.
After retiring, they decided to move to a villa in the south of Spain, where the weather was warm year-round.
The villa had a charming, rustic design, with terracotta tiles and large windows that let in the natural light.

sense.definition: a small roofed building with open sides, usually in a garden

examples.x: The couple exchanged their wedding vows under a beautifully decorated gazebo in the garden.
Every summer, the family gathers for a picnic in the gazebo, enjoying the cool shade and scenic views.
The park's centerpiece is a large, ornate gazebo where musicians often perform concerts.
She loved to spend her afternoons reading in the gazebo, surrounded by the fragrance of blooming flowers.
The new gazebo in the backyard became the perfect spot for evening tea and watching the sunset.

sense.definition: a small house separated from a larger one where guests can stay

examples.x: The guest house nestled in the garden offered a tranquil retreat for visitors, with its cozy bedrooms and private patio.
The guest house at the bed and breakfast featured charming decor and personalized service, ensuring a memorable stay for guests.
Families rented out the guest house for reunions and gatherings, enjoying the convenience of having additional space and privacy.
Tourists opted to stay in the guest house to experience local hospitality and immerse themselves in the culture of the region.
Business travelers appreciated the convenience of the guest house, with its proximity to the conference center and shuttle service to the airport.

sense.definition: a building of small size, such as a shed or garage, that shares a wall and roof with a building of larger size

examples.x: The hikers built a simple lean-to with branches and leaves to protect themselves from the unexpected rain.
In the dense forest, they discovered an old lean-to that had been used by previous campers.
The farmer stored his tools and supplies in a makeshift lean-to attached to the barn.
Seeking shelter from the wind, they huddled under a rocky overhang that acted as a natural lean-to.
The children spent the afternoon constructing a lean-to in the backyard, pretending they were explorers in the wilderness.

sense.definition: a small house in the countryside, where people temporarily stay while participating in an outdoor activity such as climbing, skiing, etc.
a round hall or building that often has a rounded roof as well
a small, simple cottage often constructed from basic materials

examples.x: The fisherman lived in a wooden shanty by the riverbank.
Families in the village built shanties out of scraps for shelter.
We stayed in a cozy beach shanty during our vacation.
Despite its rustic appearance, the shanty had a certain charm.
A small vegetable garden thrived next to the shanty.

sense.definition: a place or building that is meant to provide protection against danger or bad weather

examples.x: The hikers found shelter under a rocky overhang during the storm.
The organization provides food and shelter to the homeless.
The refugees were taken to a nearby shelter for safety.
The soldiers constructed a shelter to rest for the night.

sense.definition: a large building consisting of several apartments, particularly in a poor neighborhood

examples.x: Despite its dilapidated appearance, the tenement served as a refuge for those struggling to make ends meet, offering a semblance of stability and belonging.

sense.definition: a type of traditional dwelling made from sun-dried bricks, commonly found in arid and warm regions

examples.x: The adobe house, with its thick earthen walls, stayed cool during the hot summer days and warm during the cold nights.
They decided to renovate the old adobe house, preserving its historic charm while adding modern amenities.
The village was known for its distinctive adobe houses, each one a testament to traditional building techniques.
Inside the adobe house, the rustic wooden beams and clay floors created a cozy and inviting atmosphere.
After touring the region, they fell in love with the simplicity and beauty of the adobe house they eventually purchased.

sense.definition: a place for living in, such as a house, apartment, etc.

examples.x: The cozy dwelling nestled in the countryside was the perfect retreat for those seeking solitude and tranquility.
The urban dwelling offered modern amenities and convenience, with easy access to public transportation and city attractions.
As night fell, the flickering lights in each dwelling illuminated the quiet neighborhood, signaling the end of another day.

sense.definition: a small secondary residence, typically located in a city or urban area, that is used as a temporary or occasional dwelling
a very tall building with many floors

examples.x: The city skyline was dotted with towering high-rises, each competing for prominence.
She worked on the top floor of a high-rise, enjoying panoramic views of the city below.
The neighborhood was undergoing rapid development, with new high-rises springing up every year.
Living in a high-rise can offer convenience and amenities, but some prefer the charm of low-rise buildings.
The architect's design for the new high-rise incorporated green spaces and sustainable features.

sense.definition: a small separate building that is built near a main building that it belongs to, such as a shed or barn

examples.x: He keeps his bicycle and camping gear in the outbuilding on the property.
The family decided to turn the outbuilding into a guest house for visiting relatives.
The outbuilding was converted into a small workshop for woodworking projects.
They built an outbuilding to house their chickens and other farm animals.
She found some old furniture in the outbuilding that her grandparents had kept for years.

sense.definition: a small structure, gallery, or summerhouse that usually has an open side and provides an excellent view

examples.x: From the belvedere on the hill, they could see the entire valley spread out below them in breathtaking detail.
The estate's belvedere was a favorite spot for guests to gather and enjoy the sunset over the lake.
The architect designed a stunning belvedere that offered panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.
They enjoyed their morning coffee in the belvedere, listening to the birds and watching the sunrise.
The belvedere's open sides allowed a refreshing breeze to flow through, making it the perfect place to relax on a hot summer day.

sense.definition: a residential structure that is designed to float on water, providing a unique living environment with the possibility of mobility or stationary placement

examples.x: The family loves living in their floating home, enjoying the peaceful sound of the water every morning.
They spent the weekend on a floating home in the lake, relaxing and fishing.
Many people in coastal areas are opting for floating homes as an alternative to traditional houses.
The couple plans to retire on a floating home, cruising along the coastline for a few years.
There is a community of floating homes along the harbor, where people enjoy a unique lifestyle.

sense.definition: a house or shelter in the shape of a dome that is built from blocks of ice or hard snow

examples.x: In the Arctic, indigenous people have been building traditional igloos for generations to withstand harsh winter conditions.
The Inuit family constructed a cozy igloo using blocks of ice to protect themselves from the freezing Arctic winds.
Children in northern regions enjoy building miniature igloos out of snow during the winter months.
During the blizzard, the stranded mountaineers took refuge in an improvised igloo made from compacted snow blocks to stay warm and safe.

sense.definition: a sustainable and environmentally friendly home that is made primarily from natural and recycled materials, such as tires, bottles, and cans

examples.x: The couple decided to build an Earthship to live sustainably and reduce their environmental impact.
He toured an Earthship community, amazed by how the homes collect rainwater and generate their own power.
Living in an Earthship allows people to live off-grid, with little need for external utilities.
She planted a garden inside her Earthship to grow fresh vegetables year-round.

sense.definition: a circular portable tent, particularly used in Siberia and Mongolia

examples.x: The nomadic family moved their yurt to a new location, following the seasonal migration of their livestock.
Tourists can experience traditional Mongolian life by staying in a cozy yurt set up on the open steppe.
Despite its simple structure, the yurt was surprisingly warm and comfortable during the cold winter nights.
They decorated the inside of the yurt with colorful tapestries and traditional rugs, creating a welcoming atmosphere.

sense.definition: a boat designed for living in

examples.x: They spent their vacation on a houseboat cruising down the river.
The houseboat was anchored at the marina, offering a unique living experience.
She dreams of retiring on a houseboat and traveling from port to port.
The houseboat has all the amenities of a regular home, including a kitchen and bathroom.
They hosted a party on their houseboat, enjoying the sunset over the water.

sense.definition: an enclosed structure built among the branches of a tree to provide children with a place to play in

examples.x: She used the tree house as a quiet reading spot.
They built a cozy tree house in the backyard to enjoy reading and relaxing in the fresh air.
The couple designed their tree house with large windows, offering breathtaking views of the forest.
The kids had a blast decorating their tree house with fairy lights and colorful cushions.
The children spent the afternoon playing in their tree house, imagining it was a secret fort.

sense.definition: a tiny apartment that has only one main room

examples.x: The cozy studio featured large windows that flooded the space with natural light, making it feel larger and more inviting.
He rented a studio downtown for its proximity to his workplace and the vibrant city life.
The studio had large windows that let in plenty of natural light, creating a bright and airy atmosphere.
Despite its small size, the studio felt cozy and inviting, with comfortable furnishings and tasteful decor.

sense.definition: a multi-building or multi-unit development designed for residential purposes, often featuring shared amenities and services for the residents

examples.x: Families in the residential complex enjoy the use of a community playground and a swimming pool.
The company is planning to build a new residential complex with green spaces and walking paths.
The new residential complex offers modern apartments with beautiful views of the city.
Residents of the residential complex have access to 24-hour security for added peace of mind.

sense.definition: a tall building primarily designed and constructed for residential purposes, typically housing multiple residential units or apartments within a vertical structure

examples.x: The new residential tower in the city center offers stunning views of the skyline.
Many people prefer living in a residential tower for the convenience of being close to work and entertainment.
The residential tower has a rooftop garden where residents can relax and enjoy the outdoors.
The architect designed the residential tower with large windows to maximize natural light in every apartment.

sense.definition: a portable and temporary shelter used by nomadic communities, often made from animal hides, fabric, or woven materials

examples.x: The family lived in a nomad tent as they traveled with their livestock across the desert.
Nomad tents are designed to be lightweight and easy to pack up when it is time to move.
In some cultures, nomad tents are decorated with intricate patterns and symbols that represent the family's heritage.
The children played outside the nomad tent, enjoying the freedom of life on the move.
During their journey, they found shelter in a nomad tent that provided protection from the rain.

sense.definition: a traditional Javanese wooden house with a peaked roof and intricate carvings, commonly found in Java, Indonesia

examples.x: The joglo stood proudly in the village, its layered roof visible from afar.
Many traditional ceremonies in Java are held inside a joglo because of its spacious and open design.
The wooden beams of the joglo were intricately carved, showcasing local craftsmanship.
Visitors admired the unique structure of the joglo, especially its high, central roof.

sense.definition: a simple shelter or dwelling made by using a mixture of mud, clay, and other natural materials, commonly found in rural or traditional communities

examples.x: The farmer built a mud hut near the field to rest during the hot afternoons.
The walls of the mud hut were reinforced with straw to make them stronger.
Travelers admired the craftsmanship of the mud hut, noting how it blended with the landscape.
The family gathered inside their mud hut to share a meal after a day of work.
Inside the mud hut, the temperature remained cool despite the scorching heat outside.

sense.definition: a traditional mansion or large residence with intricate architectural designs, commonly found in South Asia

examples.x: Intricate carvings on the doors and windows made the haveli a masterpiece of traditional craftsmanship.
The courtyard of the haveli was a lively space where the family gathered for meals and celebrations.
Visitors admired the painted murals on the walls of the haveli, which depicted scenes from local folklore.

sense.definition: a traditional Moroccan house built around a central courtyard, often with ornate architecture and a peaceful atmosphere

examples.x: Staying in a riad allowed the tourists to experience Moroccan culture and hospitality firsthand.
The walls of the riad were beautifully adorned with intricate patterns and vibrant colors.
Guests enjoyed their meals under the open sky in the central courtyard of the riad.
The family spent evenings in their riad, gathered around the fountain, sharing stories and tea.
Many riads in Marrakech have been transformed into charming boutique hotels for travelers.

sense.definition: a large and strong building that is protected against attacks, in which the royal family lives

examples.x: They toured the ancient castle, exploring its grand halls and secret passages.
The castle’s architecture featured elaborate turrets and battlements, reflecting its historical importance.
During the summer vacation, the family visited several castles across Europe, each with its unique history.
The castle’s restoration project aimed to preserve its historical features while making it accessible to visitors.
He dreamed of living in a fairytale castle overlooking the sea.

sense.definition: a residence designed and built with a focus on environmental sustainability, energy efficiency, and the use of eco-friendly materials and technologies to minimize its impact on the environment

examples.x: Solar panels on the roof of the ecohouse provided much of the electricity needed for daily life.
The family chose to live in an ecohouse to reduce their carbon footprint and live more sustainably.
The use of recycled materials in the construction of the ecohouse made it an environmentally friendly option.
The ecohouse was designed with large windows to let in natural light, reducing the need for artificial lighting.
Many ecohouses are built in rural areas, offering a peaceful and sustainable way of living.

sense.definition: a building or a set of buildings for soldiers to live in

examples.x: The soldiers were assigned to newly built barracks, which provided much better living conditions than the old ones.
After a long day of training, the recruits returned to their barracks to rest and recuperate.
The historic barracks, now a museum, offer a glimpse into the daily lives of soldiers from the past.
The barracks were filled with the sounds of camaraderie as soldiers shared stories and jokes in their free time.
During the inspection, the commander praised the soldiers for maintaining such orderly and clean barracks.

sense.definition: a house, particularly found in New York City, that is built of, or with a front made of, a type of reddish-brown sandstone

examples.x: They recently purchased a charming brownstone in Brooklyn.
The historic district is famous for its row of elegant brownstones.
Her dream home was always a brownstone with a classic stoop.
He inherited the family brownstone located in the Upper West Side.
The renovation of the old brownstone took nearly a year to complete.

sense.definition: a small building with a high ceiling that is primarily used to provide a covered space for coaches and carriages when they are not in use

examples.x: The old carriage house was converted into a cozy guest cottage for visiting family and friends.
The family restored the carriage house to its original design, keeping the large doors and wooden beams.
The estate included a carriage house that was used to store vintage cars and other equipment.
They turned the carriage house into a studio, using the spacious area for painting and crafting.
The carriage house still had the tracks on the floor where the horses' carriages used to be stored.

sense.definition: a single-family house that is not connected to any other house, usually with its own yard or garden

examples.x: They moved into a spacious detached house in the countryside.
The detached house had a large garden and a driveway.
He preferred living in a detached house because it felt more secluded.
A detached house offers more privacy than a semi-detached home.
She loved the idea of having a detached house with a private backyard.
